name: San SE Ju i Chuan (Tri - Color Shrimp Rolls)
recipeInstructions:
----------------------MARINATE SHRIMP----------------------
2 Scallions
3 Ginger slices
1/2 tbsp Wine
1/2 tsp Salt
1/2 tsp Pepper
1/2 Egg white
1 tbsp Cornstarch
Procedure:
1) Sprinkle some salt on shrimp and mix well. Rinse in water and dry.
2) Pound shrimp gently with the back of a knife to mince into paste.
3) Mince pork fat and mix both in a large bowl.
4) Pound scallions, ginger, wine, salt, pepper, and egg white in a small bowl.
5) Add the above mixture to the minced shrimp. Stir until sticky and smooth.
6) Add 1 tablespoon cornstarch and stir again.
7) Soak mushrooms in warm water for 10 minutes. Drain, remove stems, and shred thinly.
8) Boil green vegetables for 3 seconds, then plunge into cold water and squeeze dry.
9) Beat eggs with salt, cornstarch mixed with water. Shape into a thin pancake (about 10 inches).
10) Spread half of the shrimp mixture evenly on the pancake and sprinkle more cornstarch.
11) Place vegetable leaves and remaining shrimp mixture on top. Add shredded mushrooms in two rows along the ends.
12) Roll the pancake toward the center, sealing with some shrimp paste. Transfer to a serving dish.
13) Steam for about 8 minutes. Remove, cut into slices, and arrange attractively.
14) Optionally, dip the rolls in a flour batter made with eggs, cornstarch, and cold water, then deep fry until golden brown before cutting.
(Note: Laver and ham shreds may be used instead of vegetable leaves and black mushrooms.)
